question: To what extent does rising housing-cost unaffordability causally increase support for right-populist or anti-establishment parties in European cities between 2015 and 2025?
background: Synthetic reference project used to implement and test the Research Method Kernel. It does not contain empirical findings.
objectives: Compile the causal question into explicit concepts, estimands, designs and claim gates.
Exercise the full method workflow using synthetic fixtures only.
non_goals: Producing a real estimate for European cities.
Using participant-identifiable or licensed restricted data.

scope.population: Synthetic representations of urban renters and city-election panels

starting_assumptions: Housing affordability is multidimensional and must not be collapsed into one unreviewed measure.
Right-populist and anti-establishment classifications require versioned local taxonomies.
quality_gates: No causal claim above the IdentificationAudit claim ceiling.
No synthetic result may be promoted to an empirical claim.
No confirmatory run before the PreAnalysisPlan is locked.
stop_conditions: Synthetic fixture escapes the test namespace
